ref: Jesus and his disciples returned to the crowd. A man knelt in front of him
text: Lefe nǝ Yesu ku wǝ bakasebi díbǝ báyo sitiditu siamǝ ɔflɔ, osani utsyuǝ ǝbǝ óse akonki lǝ Yesu ɔflɔ.
translated: The word of Jesus to the people of the church is that they should listen attentively and obey in the assembly, but they do not act according to all the teachings of Jesus in the assembly.

ref: But Jesus turned to see who had touched him.
text: Tsyaa, Yesu esi úkǝnǝ úńyǝ ɔlɛ ǝnǝmi botsyilamasǝ kabe lǝ woofo futsyǝ dítidi wǝ bunyǝ.
translated: Oh, Jesus knows the love of his heart, which is great mercy for all the little people he serves.

ref: But Jesus replied: You want a sign because you are evil and won't believe! But the only sign you will get is the sign of the prophet Jonah.
text: Mfó nya Yesu dítǝ mbuayɛ nkǝ, “Bebe, mi mmi eto betidi ba leesumu bǝfiǝ ku asa bimba. Bela biǝ biǝnyǝ ayanisa itsyi mɔ ɔflɔ, fɛɛ biembunyǝ ayanisa saa enso nnya dítsyi Onanto eto Unyɛlǝkǝtidi Yona ɔflɔ.
translated: The people said to Jesus, "Lord, we cannot do these deeds that we are able to do because we lack power. But we see that each day we can only do small things with our mouths, and we receive each day's provision only through the works of the Almighty Lord Jesus."

ref: The official got down on his knees and began begging, “Have pity on me, and I will pay you every cent I owe!”
text: “Itǝ uyifoyifo ǝmfo áse akonki lǝ oka ǝmǝ ǝnǝmi ɔ́lɛ wǝ lekpakpa ɔ́tɛyi nkǝ, ‘Tsyǝ ǝsuǝ ko mɛ, moofo fǝ kayo kamǝ kenke.’
translated: He told the assembly thus, gathering my people and those who dwell in the village homes around, "I do not hear you speak, your voice does not truly come to me."

ref: A good tree cannot produce bad fruit, and a bad tree cannot produce good fruit.
text: Kuyi sɛɛ saa aannwɔ ebibi bua nko ke kuyi bua saa laannwɔ ebibi sɛɛ nɛ.
translated: We see this, tell the people what they do, and we do this, hear the people see here.

ref: After King Herod died, an angel from the Lord appeared in a dream to Joseph while he was still in Egypt.
text: Se Oka Herodes díkpǝ eto kamaa, Onanto eto ɔtɔnkpe utsyuǝ ǝlǝkǝ ǝsuǝ óte Yosef lǝ kola kǝmiǝ lǝ Egipte.
translated: When King Herod heard about the child, he commanded Joseph to take the child and his wife and go to Egypt.

ref: Why worry about clothes? Look how the wild flowers grow. They don't work hard to make their clothes.
text: “Bibu bebe kase dituwinimi lǝ dimǝ laanɔ bobee. Kaanta olokotutu nya keendi kafia kefi keti ǝsuǝ.
translated: We say only this matter about the people's home every day. They speak false words and take the chief's with that truth.

ref: You are a bunch of evil snakes, so how can you say anything good? Your words show what is in your hearts.
text: Mimba lɛbɔ bitsyi lǝ bawa eto kafo ǝsuǝ! Asa sɛɛ aambɔ ketsyi lǝ mi kɛnyɛ! Itsyise lesa nǝ diyi mi ketu ǝsuǝǝ, nnya mi kɛnyɛ lǝǝlǝkǝ katɛyi nɛ.
translated: Hear ye, the youth who do good deeds always! But know that the wise elders I honor! Truly their words that teach me I always receive, and I honor their ways that I follow daily.

ref: Someone may say, “Here is the Messiah!” or “There he is!” But don't believe it.
text: “Nyaso lǝ utsyuǝ átɛyi mi nkǝ, ‘Bebe! Nnyǝ Nwǝ Onanto Lékpo Ɔhile Utsyǝ nkpe mfo o-o,’ nye ‘Nnyǝ wǝ nkpe mmǝǝ,’ mamfo wǝ anɔ.
translated: Nyaso and the people said to me, “Hey! Our Chief Onanto and the people have many things to say, oh,” and said “We have many words,” so they left.

ref: Peter began to curse and swear, “I don't know that man!” Right then a rooster crowed,
text: Nya Petro lɛtɛyi nkǝ, “Ekpe lǝ lesa saa bǝ ɔnukualɛ minlɛ bɔtɛyi minkǝ Onanto lǝ unǝ mɛ koto lǝ sidia minlɛ bɔtɛyi!” Lefe nǝmfo tutuutu nnya ukusɛ dítǝ kɛnyɛ.
translated: Nya Petro said to the people, "Chief, let us attend to these matters of our cultivated land; the community says we must not be lazy, let us assist our cultivated land!" Then the elders gathered together and spoke clearly, agreeing.

ref: Everyone was amazed at his teaching. He taught with authority, and not like the teachers of the Law of Moses.
text: Iyifo mba kenke lɛnɔ wǝ asatete nyamǝ yanii, itsyise ɛbɔ lete lǝ mba laate asa itsyi lǝ Yudafɔ eto afi ǝsuǝ. Bomu wǝ asatete eyifo nnya nkpe kalende nya itsyi taa wǝ utu kǝmiǝ.
translated: The story of what happened in the village where the people cultivated taro for the youths is that they decided to build a small house in the place called Yudafɔ for storing grain. But the problem the people faced during the harvest was that there was little, so that they would lack.

ref: Instead, store up your treasures in heaven, where moths and rust cannot destroy them, and thieves cannot break in and steal them.
text: Bomu bɛlɔ mi bikǝ bitsyǝ lǝ Onanto eto. Mfoo, balɛ embobiasǝ mi nye beto kosonsa nya beyu tsya lemboba siku beyu mi.
translated: People say to me that small things are in Onanto market. But they cause suffering to me, give me all bad things, and day and night without food for my part.

ref: Jesus and his disciples crossed the lake and brought the boat to shore near the town of Gennesaret.
text: Se bédufǝ ditumbukplɛ nǝmǝ eto lekpake nǝ nsee, báyifǝ lǝ Genesaret eto kasɔ. Nya bátsyasǝ mǝ ɔklɔ ǝmǝ mfó.
translated: The people gathered together from their place, having traveled far and arrived here; they went to the shore of Genesaret. But they did not catch any fish with their nets.

ref: The soldiers took the money and did what they were told. The people of Judea still tell each other this story. What Jesus' Followers Must Do (Mark
text: Bamamanyǝ bamǝ áfo atabi nyamǝ nya béyifo fe kase bátɛyi mǝ. Nyaso nyamfo ni ditiki nǝ lɛyɛ létsyilama Yudafɔ ntii taa mmi nɛ. Yesu Ǝlǝkǝ Ǝsuǝ Óte Wǝ Bakasebi.
translated: Our forefathers and the people went to battle, and their cattle enemies, those who slay, bring me trouble. So the folk in this little thing have a prophet Yudafɔ who speaks to me. Jesus, Lord God, Almighty Deliverer.

ref: The young man said, “I have obeyed all of these. What else must I do?”
text: Mfó nya okosobi ǝmfo dítǝ mbuayɛ nkǝ, “Mbuyifo lǝ afi nyamfo kenke ǝsuǝ. Be disi ibu bǝ nyifo?”
translated: Chief said the people committed an evil deed against us, "They took all their cattle? But is this the truth?"

ref: After King Herod died, an angel from the Lord appeared in a dream to Joseph while he was still in Egypt.
text: Se Oka Herodes díkpǝ eto kamaa, Onanto eto ɔtɔnkpe utsyuǝ ǝlǝkǝ ǝsuǝ óte Yosef lǝ kola kǝmiǝ lǝ Egipte.
translated: When King Herod heard about the child, he ordered that they seize the boys and their mothers, except Joseph, and Joseph flee to Egypt.

translated: It is said that we should not abandon these joint tasks. Listen, everyone, take care of your house and your field properly.

ref: You are a bunch of evil snakes, so how can you say anything good? Your words show what is in your hearts.
text: Mimba lɛbɔ bitsyi lǝ bawa eto kafo ǝsuǝ! Asa sɛɛ aambɔ ketsyi lǝ mi kɛnyɛ! Itsyise lesa nǝ diyi mi ketu ǝsuǝǝ, nnya mi kɛnyɛ lǝǝlǝkǝ katɛyi nɛ.
translated: Listen, the young ones say these words truly! But you don’t listen to my words! Because they truly make my mistakes, so I know much pain for myself.

ref: Someone may say, “Here is the Messiah!” or “There he is!” But don't believe it.
text: “Nyaso lǝ utsyuǝ átɛyi mi nkǝ, ‘Bebe! Nnyǝ Nwǝ Onanto Lékpo Ɔhile Utsyǝ nkpe mfo o-o,’ nye ‘Nnyǝ wǝ nkpe mmǝǝ,’ mamfo wǝ anɔ.
translated: Nyaso and the people said to me, “Hey! Our Chief Onanto and the people are many indeed,” and said, “We are few,” but they exist.

ref: Peter began to curse and swear, “I don't know that man!” Right then a rooster crowed,
text: Nya Petro lɛtɛyi nkǝ, “Ekpe lǝ lesa saa bǝ ɔnukualɛ minlɛ bɔtɛyi minkǝ Onanto lǝ unǝ mɛ koto lǝ sidia minlɛ bɔtɛyi!” Lefe nǝmfo tutuutu nnya ukusɛ dítǝ kɛnyɛ.
translated: Nya Petro said to the people, "The Ekpe council has decided that we build a new market square in our town, since the people of Onanto have not had enough provisions to support our town's new market!" Then the people quickly went and started the work, which they there completed.
